name: CNH Industrial N.V.

profile.description: CNH Industrial N.V. is a global capital goods company that designs, manufactures, and distributes agricultural and construction equipment under brands including Case IH, New Holland, and Case Construction. The company is executing a 'Breaking New Ground' strategy combining AI, autonomy, and precision technology with its legacy equipment to modernize farming and construction. Full-year 2025 revenue declined 9% to $18.1B, though Q4 showed 6% YoY growth. CEO Gerrit Marx leads the company through the deepest agricultural equipment downturn in decades.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): CNH has consistently introduced precision agriculture technology with over 15 new tractor launches, 10 harvesting platforms, and 30+ precision tech releases planned through 2027 [1]. The 2022 Iveco Group spinoff was a transformative strategic simplification that created a pure-play agriculture/construction company. AI Deployment (2/2): CNH's 2025 Tech Day showcased an 'AI Tech Assistant' deployed to hundreds of dealer groups for real-time diagnostic support, with CEO Marx confirming every member of the global leadership team runs at least one generative or agentic AI pilot program [2]. CTO Friedrich Eichler (ex-automotive) leads innovation in automation, digitalization, and electrification. Market Share (1/2): CNH holds meaningful global position in agricultural equipment alongside Deere and AGCO, with strong brand equity through Case IH and New Holland, but is not the category leader. Product Pipeline (1/2): 2027 product roadmap has specific named launches and precision tech releases, providing visibility though execution remains uncertain in the downturn [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending is slightly below peer leader John Deere as a percentage of revenue.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): CNH's 'Breaking New Ground' purpose is specific and thoughtful — it connects equipment manufacturing to the challenge of feeding a growing world sustainably [2]. CEO Marx has explicitly framed food security and farming productivity as generational missions, and the AI deployment strategy traces directly to this purpose. The mission differentiates from peers and shapes R&D investment even in a down cycle. Community Impact (0/2): CNH maintains employee foundations and corporate philanthropy but no distinctive community programs were identified at scale. Employee Retention (1/2): The 2024 leadership reorganization under CEO Marx (appointed July 2024) created the new Global Leadership Team with experienced internal talent, suggesting organizational stability, though the restructuring itself adds transition uncertainty [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): No significant net insider buying by executives identified in the last 12 months; the challenging earnings environment (EPS declining from $1.05 to $0.55) provides context.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically calculated. Liquidity check passes: $10.15 × 15,745,800 avg daily volume = $159.8M [4]. Growth stock scoring: D/E of 333.7% exceeds the 300% floor (score 0.0, 1× weight). ROE of 4.84% is well below 15% target (score 0.32, 2× weight). Revenue growth YoY of -0.1% is near-flat after a 9% decline (score 0.66, 1× weight). Revenue growth 5yr CAGR estimated at ~2% (score 0.47, 2× weight). Forward P/E of 13.9 is well below 20 target (score 1.0, 1× weight). Profit margin of 2.1% is thin for an industrial (score 0.11, 1× weight). Forward PEG of 0.59 is excellent, below 1.0 target (score 1.0, 2× weight). Weighted sum 5.35 / max 10 × 10 = 5.4, rounded to 5 [1][4]. The extremely low PEG and forward P/E reflect expected earnings recovery from cyclical trough.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): CNH manufactures physical heavy equipment — tractors, combines, harvesters, construction machinery — that cannot be replaced by AI software [1]. The business involves capital-intensive physical products with significant aftermarket services, dealer networks, and financing arms, all structurally protected from AI substitution. Farming and construction require physical-world operations that AI structurally cannot displace. AI is an enabler: precision agriculture tools, AI-powered field analytics, autonomous equipment capabilities, and the AI Tech Assistant enhance product value and customer retention [2]. However, John Deere's more aggressive autonomous equipment and AI deployment timeline creates competitive pressure — CNH risks falling behind in AI-enabled precision agriculture if execution lags, which would erode market share without replacing the physical equipment moat. AI Opportunity (1/1): The AI Tech Assistant is deployed across dealer networks generating measurable diagnostic support value, and every global leader runs AI pilots — qualifying as meaningful AI revenue contribution in the agricultural equipment context [2].

infinite_game.summary: CNH has a genuine long-term purpose around sustainable agriculture and food security that shapes R&D investment and product strategy even through a severe cyclical downturn. The Iveco spinoff demonstrated willingness to fundamentally reshape the company in service of strategic focus. However, the company's downturn response has been cautious — managing costs and protecting margins rather than making the kind of bold, costly bets Sinek would describe as courage to lead.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): CNH's 'Breaking New Ground' purpose connects equipment manufacturing to feeding a growing world sustainably. CEO Marx has framed AI deployment explicitly in service of this cause — 'tomorrow's agronomist will be agentic AI helping farmers make bigger decisions better' [2]. The purpose is lived through sustained R&D investment (15+ tractor launches, 30+ precision tech releases) even during the worst agricultural equipment downturn in decades. However, the purpose has not been tested through a true existential crisis or multi-decade consistency under multiple CEOs.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CNH's 2024 Global Leadership Team reorganization under CEO Marx assembled experienced internal talent in a simplified matrix structure, suggesting collaborative intent [2]. The company's 11 consecutive years in Dow Jones Sustainability Indices and strong ESG ratings indicate organizational health [3]. However, the recent leadership transition (Marx appointed July 2024) and agricultural downturn creating potential restructuring pressure add uncertainty. Evidence is adequate but not exceptional.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): CNH explicitly benchmarks against John Deere, AGCO, and Caterpillar, framing its product roadmap in terms of capability gaps to close rather than competitors to defeat [2]. Management language focuses on delivering better technology and improving farmer outcomes. The acknowledgment that Deere leads in certain autonomous and AI capabilities motivates CNH's own precision agriculture investment rather than triggering defensive posturing.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): The 2022 Iveco Group spinoff was a meaningful strategic simplification — CNH divested its entire truck and commercial vehicle business to become a pure-play agriculture/construction company [1]. The ongoing shift from pure iron manufacturer to integrated precision agriculture technology platform also represents strategic evolution. However, neither pivot was transformative at the level Sinek describes — the core agricultural equipment identity remained unchanged.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Despite the worst agricultural equipment downturn in decades, CNH maintained its full technology investment roadmap — 15+ tractor launches, 30+ precision tech releases through 2027 — choosing long-term capability over short-term cost cuts [1][2]. CEO Marx's statement that 'CNH is moving fast to deliver commitments even as markets move slowly' reflects commitment to investing through the cycle. However, Q1 2026 guidance of near break-even results and cost savings of $230M suggest the company is also managing defensively.

investment_thesis: CNH Industrial is a high-quality agricultural equipment franchise navigating a cyclical trough with visible recovery catalysts. Q4 2025 delivered 6% revenue growth and 21% YoY industrial adjusted EBIT improvement, demonstrating the operating leverage that will drive earnings recovery [1]. At 13.9x forward P/E and 0.59x PEG ratio, the stock is priced for trough-level earnings ($0.32 trailing EPS) with analysts forecasting $0.73 forward EPS — a 128% earnings recovery. CNH's precision agriculture technology stack — with 30+ new product releases planned by 2027 and AI Tech Assistant deployed across dealer networks — positions it to capture greater value per unit as the cycle turns [2]. The Inspire Impact score of 64 (ALIGNED) reflects exceptional ESG practices across 17 positive categories [3].
thesis_continued: The bear case is severe for near-term holders: industry retail demand is forecast down another 5% in 2026 to historic trough levels, with high-horsepower North American tractors facing 15-20% demand declines [1]. Adjusted EPS is guided $0.35-$0.45 for 2026, down from $0.55 in 2025 and $1.05 in 2024 — earnings are still deteriorating. D/E of 333.7% is well above the 300% floor, reflecting the financial services arm's leverage that amplifies risk in a prolonged downturn. Profit margin of 2.1% and ROE of 4.8% are well below IOWN targets. Tariff costs created 110bps of EBIT margin headwind in 2025 with expectations for 2026 to worsen. The agricultural recovery thesis requires farmer income stabilization and commodity price support that may not materialize until 2027-2028.
key_catalysts: Agricultural equipment demand recovery beginning in 2027 as dealer inventory destocking completes and farmer income stabilizes, unlocking significant operating leverage from CNH's current trough production levels

Precision agriculture technology monetization — AI Tech Assistant adoption driving software/service revenue above hardware margins, and autonomous equipment capabilities creating differentiation against Chinese imports
Earnings recovery trajectory from $0.32 trailing EPS to $0.73 forward consensus, which at 13.9x forward P/E implies the stock is pricing significant upside if the recovery materializes on schedule
key_risks: Agricultural equipment demand trough extending beyond 2026 as weak commodity prices and elevated interest rates delay farmer capital expenditure, creating further earnings and margin pressure

D/E of 333.7% (reflecting financial services leverage) amplifies risk in a prolonged downturn — if dealer inventory financing deteriorates, the balance sheet becomes a liability rather than a competitive advantage

John Deere and AGCO accelerating autonomous and AI-powered equipment rollouts, positioning competitors ahead of CNH in precision agriculture and risking permanent market share loss in the technology transition
